title: Sweet Tender Lazy-Sunday Toffee Popcorn Stir-fry
description: A sweet and tender Japanese-inspired dish featuring toffee popcorn as the star ingredient, combined with toast for a low-carb friendly twist.

ingredients.name: Toaster Strudels
ingredients.notes: These are used as toffee popcorn. Be mindful of portion size.

ingredients.name: Toast (Whole Grain)
ingredients.notes: Use a toaster or pan for a low-carb friendly option.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce (Low Sodium)
ingredients.notes: A touch of umami to enhance the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic Powder
ingredients.notes: To add depth of flavor without excess salt.

ingredients.name: Vegetable Oil
ingredients.notes: For cooking and adding to the stir-fry mixture.

instructions: Preheat your oven or toaster to a temperature suitable for making Toaster Strudels (about 350°F).
Slice both slices of Toast into small pieces.
Melt Butter in a non-stick pan and add sliced Toast. Cook until lightly golden brown, stirring occasionally.
While Toast is cooking, pop and cool the Toaster Strudels as per instructions on packaging.
In a wok or large skillet, heat Vegetable Oil over medium-high heat. Add Garlic Powder and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add cooked Toast to the oil in the wok/pan, stirring continuously until toast is well coated with oil.
Gradually add cooled Toaster Strudels to the pan/wok, stirring gently to ensure even distribution of ingredients, especially Toffee Popcorn chunks. This step should take about 1 minute.
Drizzle Soy Sauce over the stir-fry mixture and continue cooking for another minute until sauce is well incorporated into the dish.
Remove from heat immediately after adding soy sauce for best texture of Toffee Popcorn.
Serve hot with a side of lemon wedges or low-fat yogurt if desired.
